content.content:
6ca7d37 docs move uninstall command to readme uninstall section (#435)
bf0642c Fix uninstall.sh for curl-to-bash execution (#426)
ffdccaf ci: add nightly E2E with inference test (#386)
12fc0c0 docs: add uninstall section to quickstart in README (#423)
60992be docs: add discord channel to docs site (#416)
---
a0db175 Add Google Tasks API to network policy and configure Entire CLI
52722a1 New setup commands, per-user credential isolation, and multi-user hardening
5e66ae0 Google OAuth persistence: proactive refresh and token backup
287958c Slack bridge: DM-only routing and auth error redaction
e089e2d Fix GitHub setup: create /sandbox/.config/gh/ dir before writing hosts.yml

52722a1 New setup commands, per-user credential isolation, and multi-user hardening

content.content:
eb4ba8c fix: restore routed inference and connect UX (#1037)
5f692e5 fix(policies): preset application for versionless policies (Fixes #35) (#101)
c051cbb fix(sandbox): export proxy env vars with full NO_PROXY and persist across reconnects (#1025)
6f9d530 fix(security): strip credentials from migration snapshots and enforce blueprint digest (#769)
a03eda0 fix: harden installer and onboard resiliency (#961)

content.text: 7 conflicts to resolve. Let me look at each one.

input.prompt:
We have 7 merge conflicts to resolve from merging main into multi-user-claude branch. The multi-user-claude branch has custom multi-user additions that should be preserved on top of the upstream changes.

The conflicted files are:
1. .gitignore
2. Dockerfile
3. bin/nemoclaw.js
4. nemoclaw-blueprint/policies/presets/npm.yaml
5. nemoclaw-blueprint/policies/presets/slack.yaml
6. scripts/nemoclaw-start.sh
7. scripts/start-services.sh

For each file:
1. Read the file to see the conflict markers
2. Resolve by keeping BOTH upstream changes AND our multi-user additions. The upstream (main) changes are the base, and our multi-user-claude additions should be layered on top.
3. Edit the file to remove conflict markers with the correct resolution.

After resolving all conflicts, run `git add` on all resolved files.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT commit. Just resolve and stage.

content.text:
All 7 conflicted files are resolved and staged. Here is a summary of the resolutions:

1. **`.gitignore`** -- Kept upstream's organized structure (build artifacts, OS metadata, security sections) and appended our multi-user persist paths (`persist/gateway`, `persist/users`, `persist/workspace/memory/`, etc.) at the bottom.

2. **`Dockerfile`** -- Kept upstream's builder stage (`COPY --from=builder`) and added our multi-user tooling installs (Claude Code CLI, gh, gog, xurl, PyYAML). Kept upstream's dynamic providers/gateway/channels config structure and merged in our Anthropic provider definition. Preserved our pre-created `.openclaw` directories.

3. **`bin/nemoclaw.js`** -- Merged both sets of global commands: upstream's `debug`, `--version`, `-v` and our `user-add`, `user-remove`, `user-purge`, `user-list`, `user-status`. Applied same merge to help text and switch/case dispatch.

4. **`nemoclaw-blueprint/policies/presets/npm.yaml`** -- Kept upstream's `access: full` style and broader binary globs (`npm*`, `npx*`, `node*`, `yarn*`), added our `openclaw` binary.

5. **`nemoclaw-blueprint/policies/presets/slack.yaml`** -- Kept upstream's WebSocket endpoints (`wss-primary.slack.com`, `wss-backup.slack.com`) and added our additional binaries (`claude`, `openclaw`, `curl`).

6. **`scripts/nemoclaw-start.sh`** -- Replaced HEAD's orphaned Python config code with upstream's `verify_config_integrity()` function. Kept our credential injection functions (`inject_claude_credentials`, `inject_github_token`, `inject_google_credentials`, `inject_gog_credentials`, `inject_slack_webhook`, `inject_gog_keyring_password`, `update_anthropic_apikey_in_config`) alongside upstream's proxy environment setup. Added credential injection calls in the Main section.

7. **`scripts/start-services.sh`** -- Kept our multi-user Slack bridge logic (auto-selects `slack-bridge-multi.js` when `users.json` exists) and added upstream's Telegram bridge and cloudflared tunnel support.

content.text: No conflict markers remain. Clean merge state. Ready to commit.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Merge upstream/main into multi-user-claude

Syncs 135 upstream commits while preserving multi-user additions:
credential injection, user management CLI, multi-user Slack bridge,
and custom network policy entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Done. Merged 135 upstream commits into `multi-user-claude` with all 7 conflicts resolved:

| File | Resolution |
|------|-----------|
| `.gitignore` | Upstream structure + our persist paths |
| `Dockerfile` | Upstream builder stage + our multi-user tooling |
| `bin/nemoclaw.js` | Both command sets (upstream debug + our user-*) |
| `npm.yaml` / `slack.yaml` | Upstream policies + our extra binaries |
| `nemoclaw-start.sh` | Upstream config integrity + our credential injection |
| `start-services.sh` | Our multi-user Slack bridge + upstream Telegram/cloudflared |
